iala-buoyage.title: IALA Region A buoyage: reconstruct a mark instead of memorising it
iala-buoyage.body:
In Region A (Europe, incl. France), buoyage encodes the side of the channel through COLOUR and SHAPE — so you can work out any mark rather than learning a list by heart.

The reference direction is "coming from seaward" (from the sea towards the harbour):
— Port hand: RED mark, CYLINDRICAL (can) shape; leave it on your left when entering.
— Starboard hand: GREEN mark, CONICAL shape; leave it on your right when entering.
The shape repeats the side: even against the light or at night, a cone is still "starboard" and a can "port".

Other marks don't edge a channel — they qualify a point:
— Safe water (mid-channel / landfall): VERTICAL red and white stripes, red spherical topmark — no danger, pass either side.
— Special mark: all YELLOW (yellow St Andrew's cross) — marks a zone or installation (bathing, submerged pipe, restricted area), not a danger in itself.

The practical rule fits in one sentence: a lateral mark's colour and shape tell you which side to pass — once you know which way is "from seaward".

prov.ref: Balisage IALA région A — marques latérales, eaux saines, marques spéciales
prov.source: IALA — Système de balisage maritime, région A (Recommandation R1001)
